docs.title: GeniXCMS Documentation
docs.text: GeniXCMS is a PHP-based Content Management System and Framework — lightweight, extensible, and developer-friendly. Built on Bootstrap and powered by a Latte templating engine, it provides all the tools you need to build blogs, websites, and custom web applications.

docs.title: Quick Navigation
docs.text: 🚀 Installation Get GeniXCMS up and running on your server in minutes. ⚙️ Configuration Set up your site name, email, permalink, and language preferences. 📝 Managing Posts Create, edit, publish, and bulk-manage your blog content. 🎨 Create a Theme Build a custom theme using Latte templates and hooks. 🔌 Create a Module Extend GeniXCMS with custom modules and frontend pages. 🚀 Asset Bundling Modern theme development with Vite, SCSS, and Tailwind. 🗃️ Query Builder Fluent interface for database operations. 🏘️ ORM / Model Active Record layer for object-based database access. 🚀 Migration System CLI-driven database versioning tool. 📡 REST API (v1) Headless CMS (External) capabilities. 🛡️ Security Infrastructure Content Security Policy (CSP) and hardening headers. 🏗️ DI Container Service registration and Dependency Injection. 📖 API Reference Explore the full PHP class library used internally by the CMS.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text: Feature Description Latte Templating Fast, secure template engine with auto-escaping and translator support Smart URL / Permalink SEO-friendly URL routing with regex-based path matching Multi-language (i18n) Built-in language switching and locale management Hook System Actions & Filters for non-invasive extensibility Output Caching File-based and Redis page caching CSRF Protection Built-in token validation on all forms Security Headers Default Content Security Policy (CSP) and hardening RESTful API Programmatic access for mobile/SPA apps Media Management Secure file uploads, image thumbnails, WebP conversion ACL System Fine-grained, group-based Access Control Matrix Module System Drop-in modules with admin panels and frontend pages Theme System Full-featured theme engine with options panel support

docs.title: Privacy Policy
docs.text:
This privacy policy has been compiled to better serve those who are concerned with how their 'Personally Identifiable Information' (PII) is being used online. PII, as described in US privacy law and information security, is information that can be used on its own or with other information to identify, contact, or locate a single person, or to identify an individual in context. Please read our privacy policy carefully to get a clear understanding of how we collect, use, protect or otherwise handle your Personally Identifiable Information in accordance with our website.

What personal information do we collect from the people that visit our blog, website or app? When ordering or registering on our site, as appropriate, you may be asked to enter your name, email address, phone number or other details to help you with your experience.

docs.title: When do we collect information?
docs.text: We collect information from you when you register on our site or enter information on our site.

docs.title: How do we use your information?
docs.text: We may use the information we collect from you when you register, make a purchase, sign up for our newsletter, respond to a survey or marketing communication, surf the website, or use certain other site features in the following ways: • To personalize your experience and to allow us to deliver the type of content and product offerings in which you are most interested.

docs.title: How do we protect your information?
docs.text:
• We do not use vulnerability scanning and/or scanning to PCI standards.
  • We only provide articles and information. We never ask for credit card numbers.
  • We use regular Malware Scanning.
  • Your personal information is contained behind secured networks and is only accessible by a limited number of persons who have special access rights to such systems, and are required to keep the information confidential. In addition, all sensitive/credit information you supply is encrypted via Secure Socket Layer (SSL) technology.
  • We implement a variety of security measures when a user enters, submits, or accesses their information to maintain the safety of your personal information. All transactions are processed through a gateway provider and are not stored or processed on our servers.

docs.title: Do we use 'cookies'?
docs.text:
Yes. Cookies are small files that a site or its service provider transfers to your computer's hard drive through your Web browser (if you allow) that enables the site's or service provider's systems to recognize your browser and capture and remember certain information. For instance, we use cookies to help us remember and process the items in your shopping cart. They are also used to help us understand your preferences based on previous or current site activity, which enables us to provide you with improved services. We also use cookies to help us compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interaction so that we can offer better site experiences and tools in the future.

We use cookies to:

  • Help remember and process the items in the shopping cart.
  • Understand and save user's preferences for future visits.
  • Keep track of advertisements.
  • Compile aggregate data about site traffic and site interactions in order to offer better site experiences and tools in the future. We may also use trusted third-party services that track this information on our behalf.

You can choose to have your computer warn you each time a cookie is being sent, or you can choose to turn off all cookies. You do this through your browser settings. Since browser is a little different, look at your browser's Help Menu to learn the correct way to modify your cookies.

If you turn cookies off, some features will be disabled. It won't affect the user's experience that make your site experience more efficient and may not function properly.

However, you will still be able to place orders .

docs.title: Third-party disclosure
docs.text: We do not sell, trade, or otherwise transfer to outside parties your Personally Identifiable Information.

docs.title: Third-party links
docs.text: Occasionally, at our discretion, we may include or offer third-party products or services on our website. These third-party sites have separate and independent privacy policies. We therefore have no responsibility or liability for the content and activities of these linked sites. Nonetheless, we seek to protect the integrity of our site and welcome any feedback about these sites.

docs.title: Google
docs.text: Google's advertising requirements can be summed up by Google's Advertising Principles. They are put in place to provide a positive experience for users. https://support.google.com/adwordspolicy/answer/1316548?hl=en

docs.title: We use Google AdSense Advertising on our website.
docs.text:
Google, as a third-party vendor, uses cookies to serve ads on our site. Google's use of the DART cookie enables it to serve ads to our users based on previous visits to our site and other sites on the Internet. Users may opt-out of the use of the DART cookie by visiting the Google Ad and Content Network privacy policy.

We have implemented the following:

  • Remarketing with Google AdSense
  • Demographics and Interests Reporting
  • DoubleClick Platform Integration

We, along with third-party vendors such as Google use first-party cookies (such as the Google Analytics cookies) and third-party cookies (such as the DoubleClick cookie) or other third-party identifiers together to compile data regarding user interactions with ad impressions and other ad service functions as they relate to our website.

docs.title: Opting out:
docs.text: Users can set preferences for how Google advertises to you using the Google Ad Settings page. Alternatively, you can opt out by visiting the Network Advertising Initiative Opt Out page or by using the Google Analytics Opt Out Browser add on.

docs.title: California Online Privacy Protection Act
docs.text: CalOPPA is the first state law in the nation to require commercial websites and online services to post a privacy policy. The law's reach stretches well beyond California to require any person or company in the United States (and conceivably the world) that operates websites collecting Personally Identifiable Information from California consumers to post a conspicuous privacy policy on its website stating exactly the information being collected and those individuals or companies with whom it is being shared. - See more at: http://consumercal.org/california-online-privacy-protection-act-caloppa/#sthash.0FdRbT51.dpuf

docs.title: According to CalOPPA, we agree to the following:
docs.text:
Users can visit our site anonymously. Once this privacy policy is created, we will add a link to it on our home page or as a minimum, on the first significant page after entering our website. Our Privacy Policy link includes the word 'Privacy' and can be easily be found on the page specified above.

You will be notified of any Privacy Policy changes:

  • On our Privacy Policy Page

Can change your personal information:

  • By logging in to your account

docs.title: How does our site handle Do Not Track signals?
docs.text: We honor Do Not Track signals and Do Not Track, plant cookies, or use advertising when a Do Not Track (DNT) browser mechanism is in place.

docs.title: Does our site allow third-party behavioral tracking?
docs.text: It's also important to note that we allow third-party behavioral tracking

docs.title: COPPA (Children Online Privacy Protection Act)
docs.text: When it comes to the collection of personal information from children under the age of 13 years old, the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) puts parents in control. The Federal Trade Commission, United States' consumer protection agency, enforces the COPPA Rule, which spells out what operators of websites and online services must do to protect children's privacy and safety online.

docs.title: We do not specifically market to children under the age of 13 years old.
docs.text:
Fair Information Practices The Fair Information Practices Principles form the backbone of privacy law in the United States and the concepts they include have played a significant role in the development of data protection laws around the globe. Understanding the Fair Information Practice Principles and how they should be implemented is critical to comply with the various privacy laws that protect personal information.

In order to be in line with Fair Information Practices we will take the following responsive action, should a data breach occur: We will notify you via email

  • Within 7 business days

We will notify the users via in-site notification

  • Within 1 business day

We also agree to the Individual Redress Principle which requires that individuals have the right to legally pursue enforceable rights against data collectors and processors who fail to adhere to the law. This principle requires not only that individuals have enforceable rights against data users, but also that individuals have recourse to courts or government agencies to investigate and/or prosecute non-compliance by data processors.

docs.title: CAN SPAM Act
docs.text: The CAN-SPAM Act is a law that sets the rules for commercial email, establishes requirements for commercial messages, gives recipients the right to have emails stopped from being sent to them, and spells out tough penalties for violations.

docs.title: We collect your email address in order to:
docs.text:
• Send information, respond to inquiries, and/or other requests or questions
  • Market to our mailing list or continue to send emails to our clients after the original transaction has occurred.

docs.title: To be in accordance with CANSPAM, we agree to the following:
docs.text:
• Not use false or misleading subjects or email addresses.
  • Identify the message as an advertisement in some reasonable way.
  • Include the physical address of our business or site headquarters.
  • Monitor third-party email marketing services for compliance, if one is used.
  • Honor opt-out/unsubscribe requests quickly.
  • Allow users to unsubscribe by using the link at the bottom of each email.

If at any time you would like to unsubscribe from receiving future emails, follow the instructions at the bottom of each email. and we will promptly remove you from ALL correspondence.

docs.title: Contacting Us
docs.text:
If there are any questions regarding this privacy policy, you may contact us using the information below.

docs.title: About GeniXCMS
docs.text: GeniXCMS is a PHP Based Content Management System and Framework (CMSF). It's a simple and lightweight of CMSF. Very suitable for Intermediate PHP developer to Advanced Developer. Even so, GeniXCMS can be used as ordinary website like blog, portals, company profile etc. It's built with a very user friendly themes to modify.

docs.title: Why GeniXCMS
docs.text:
This CMSF is a starter point to build your own online applications. With already build User manager, Content manager (Post, Pages), Menu manager, etc made you easy to add your own code and build your own custom web applications.

Even when You are not a programmer, you can build your own website using it. Very easy to manage.

docs.title: Acl Class Documentation
docs.text: The Acl (Access Control List) class provides a system for managing granular permissions within GeniXCMS. It allows administrators to define exactly what each user group can access or do in the system.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Basic Concepts
  • How It Works
  • Method Reference
  • Programmable ACL (For Modules)
  • Registering Core Permissions

docs.title: Basic Concepts
docs.text:
Access control in GeniXCMS is divided into Resources (the thing being accessed) and Permissions (the specific action allowed on that resource).

  • Group ID: A numeric identifier for user levels (0 = Administrator, 1 = Supervisor, ..., 6 = Member).
  • Permission Key: A unique string identifying a capability (e.g., POSTS_ADD).
  • Status: Boolean (Allowed/Denied).

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Acl::check('PERMISSION_KEY') process flow: 1. Superadmin Check: If the current user is an Administrator (Level 0), access is always guaranteed. 2. Database Check: Search the permissions table for an explicit override for the current user's group. 3. Default Fallback: If no entry exists in the database, the system uses the default allowed groups defined during the permission's registration.

docs.title: static Acl::check($permission)
docs.text: Performs a permission check for the currently logged-in user. - Parameters: $permission (string) - The unique permission key. - Returns: boolean - True if allowed, false if denied.

docs.title: static Acl::register($key, $label, $default_groups = [0, 1])
docs.text: Registers a new permission capability into the system. This makes it available in the ACL Manager UI. - Parameters: - $key (string): Unique identifier (Convention: Uppercase with underscores, e.g., MYMOD_ACTIVATE). - $label (string): Human-readable description displayed in admin dashboard. - $default_groups (array): Array of group IDs that have access if no manual override is set.

docs.title: Programmable ACL (For Modules)
docs.text: To contribute new permissions from your module, register them in your module's initialization or within a hook.

docs.title: Example Registration
docs.text:
In your module's init or backend controller:

// Register a new capability
Acl::register('E_COMMERCE_DELETE_ORDER', 'Delete high-value orders', [0, 1]);

docs.title: Checking Permission in Module Logic
docs.text:
Before performing a sensitive action in your module controller:

if (Acl::check('E_COMMERCE_DELETE_ORDER')) {
    // Proceed with deletion logic...
    Order::delete($id);
    echo "Order deleted successfully.";
} else {
    // Show error or redirect
    echo "Error: You do not have permission to delete orders.";
}

docs.title: Checking in Theme/UI
docs.text:
You can also hide UI elements (buttons/links) based on permissions:

<?php if (Acl::check('E_COMMERCE_DELETE_ORDER')): ?>
    <button class="btn btn-danger">Delete Order</button>
<?php endif; ?>

docs.title: Implementation Details
docs.text:
The ACL system uses a dedicated database table permissions: - group_id: Integer identifying the user level. - permission: String identifier for the capability. - status: Tinyint (1 for allow, 0 for deny).

[!IMPORTANT] Always use Acl::check() rather than manual User::access() for new features, as it provides a much more flexible and user-manageable security layer.

docs.title: Archives Class
docs.text:
# filename: Archives.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 1.1.0
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Archives class manages the categorization and retrieval of posts by their publication date (month and year). It includes a caching mechanism for archive lists to optimize database performance.

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text:
• Dynamic Archive Listing: Generates lists of months/years where content was published.
  • Automated Caching: Stores the archive structure in a JSON-encoded system option, which is automatically updated every 24 hours.
  • Post Support: Currently optimized for content of type 'post'.

docs.title: List Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Archives::list(int $max, string $type = 'post');

Returns an HTML <ul> list of links to monthly archives. - It reads data from the archives_list system option. - Automatically formats month names using the Date::monthName() utility.

docs.title: Generate Method
docs.text:
Usage: Archives::generate(string $type = 'post');

Triggers an update to the archive cache if more than 24 hours have passed since the last update.

docs.title: Validate Method
docs.text:
Usage: Archives::validate(int $month, int $year);

Return: bool

Checks if any posts actually exist for the given month and year. This is used by the router to prevent empty archive pages.

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text:
1. Extraction: The fetchData() method queries the database using GROUP BY Month(date), Year(date) to find all unique publication periods.
  2. Persistence: The resulting structure is saved as a JSON string in the archives_list option.
  3. Trigger: The class constructor calls generate() on every request to ensure the cache stays fresh without requiring manual intervention.

docs.title: BaseControl Class
docs.text:
# filename: BaseControl.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 2.0.0-alpha
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The BaseControl is an abstract class that serves as the foundation for all frontend and backend controller classes in GeniXCMS. It provides a standardized way to initialize the Latte templating engine, populate common global data, and render theme views.

Important

Because this is an abstract class, it cannot be instantiated directly. All page controllers (e.g., FrontControl, AdminControl) must extend BaseControl.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
When a controller extends BaseControl, the constructor automatically performs two tasks:

  1. initLatte(): Sets up the Latte template engine instance, including language support and custom filters.
  2. initCommonData(): Populates the $this->data array with global variables available to every template.

docs.title: Template Engine (Latte)
docs.text: The initLatte() method configures the Latte engine with: - RawPhpExtension: Allows raw PHP execution in templates. - TranslatorExtension: Hooks into the Typo::translate() function for i18n support. - nl2br filter: Converts newlines to HTML <br> tags. - stripHtml filter: Strips HTML tags from a string. - Temp Directory: Compiled templates are cached in GX_CACHE/temp.

docs.title: Common Data (initCommonData)
docs.text:
The following variables are automatically available in every Latte template:

Variable Source Description $website_lang Options 2-letter language code $site_name Site::$name The website's title $site_url Site::$url The base URL $site_cdn Site::$cdn CDN URL, if configured $site_logo Site::logo() Pre-rendered logo HTML $theme_url Url::theme() URL to the active theme folder $token TOKEN CSRF token for forms $tag_cloud Tags::cloud() Rendered tag cloud HTML $archives_list Archives::list(10) Rendered archive links HTML $platform_version System::v() The current CMS version string

docs.title: Render Method
docs.text:
Usage: $this->render(string $view, array $data = []);

This is the core output method for any controller extending BaseControl. It: 1. Merges controller-specific $data with the common data array. 2. Generates SEO meta tags via Site::meta($this->data). 3. Starts the Cache buffer. 4. Renders the theme's header.php, then the specified $view.php, then footer.php using Latte. 5. Ends the Cache buffer, storing the output if caching is enabled.

class FrontControl extends BaseControl
{
    public function index($data)
    {
        $data['p_type'] = 'index';
        $this->render('index', $data);
    }
}

docs.title: Cache Class
docs.text:
# filename: Cache.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 1.1.2
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Cache class provides page-level output caching to improve website performance. it supports both file-based and Redis-backed caching strategies.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Caching is configured via the system settings in the admin dashboard: - cache_enabled: Set to 'on' to enable the cache. - cache_type: 'file' (default) or 'redis'. - cache_path: Directory for file-based cache. - cache_timeout: TTL for cached items in seconds (default: 3600). - redis_*: Connection details for Redis (host, port, pass, db).

docs.title: Start Method
docs.text:
Usage: Cache::start();

Call this method at the very beginning of the page execution (before any output). It: 1. Checks if a valid cached version of the current URL exists. 2. If found, it outputs the cache content and terminates the script immediately. 3. If not found, it starts output buffering (ob_start).

docs.title: End Method
docs.text:
Usage: Cache::end();

Call this method at the very end of the page execution. It: 1. Captures the buffered output content. 2. Stores the content in the cache (file or Redis) with the configured timeout. 3. Flushes the buffer to the browser.

docs.title: Logic
docs.text: The cache key is uniquely generated based on the current URL (host + request URI + query string) using an MD5 hash. This ensures that different pages and different query parameters result in separate cache entries.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• File Cache: Requires write permissions to the configured cache_path.
  • Redis Cache: Requires the php-redis extension to be installed and enabled on the server.

docs.title: Categories Class
docs.text:
# filename: Categories.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

This class is to manage the Categories. This category structure is very simple.

An improvement probably added in the future. Below are the explanation each method at the Categories Class.

docs.title: Dropdown Method
docs.text:
Usage : echo Categories::dropdown(array $vars);

Return: string

This method to create an automatic dropdown options from available categories at the database. It means we can create dropdown so easy, just fill in the parameters and a select input created automatically.

Example:

$vars = array(
             'name'      =>  'catname',
             'parent'    =>  'parent',
             'order_by'  =>  '',
             'sort'      =>  'ASC',
           )
echo Categories::dropdown($vars);

That methods calls will create output like this.

<select name="catname" class="form-control">
    <option></option>
    <option value="1">Category Name</option>
    ...
</select>

Explanation:

  • name, this is the name of the select input.
  • parent, this is the parent of the category you want to show. No parent or empty means all categories will shows up.
  • order_by, this is how you want the category ordered by. Available columns are : id, name, slug, parent, desc default is id
  • sort, this is the options how your categories sorted, Ascending ASC, or Descending DESC. Default is ASC

See also:

  • Db::result()

docs.title: Lists Method
docs.text:
Usage : echo Categories::lists(array $vars)

This method will show the list of the Categories and Subcategories with the unordered list markup.

example: 

$vars = array(
             'parent'    =>  'parent',
             'order_by'  =>  '',
             'sort'      =>  'ASC',
           )
echo Categories::lists($vars);

Explanation:

  • parent, this is the parent of the category you want to show. No parent or empty means all categories will shows up.
  • order_by, this is how you want the category ordered by. Available columns are : id, name, slug, parent, desc default is id
  • sort, this is the options how your categories sorted, Ascending ASC, or Descending DESC. Default is ASC

See also:

  • Db::result()

docs.title: Name Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Categories::name(int '$id');

Return: string

This method will get the name of Category for the specific ID.

example:

we have a list of categories with sample below :

ID Name 1 News 2 Article 
$catName = Categories::name(1);
echo $catName;

this will output News.

$catName = Categories::name(2);
echo $catName;

this will output Article.

See also:

  • Db::result()

docs.title: getParent Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Categories::getParent(int '$id');

Return: int

This is to get the Parent ID of a speicific Category ID.

example:

we have a list of categories with sample below :

ID Name Parent 1 News 0 2 Article 0 3 Latest 1 
$parent = Categories::getParent(3);
echo $parent;

this will output 1.

See also:

  • Db::result()

docs.title: Delete Method
docs.text:
Usage: Categories::delete(int '$id');

Return: bool

This method will delete a Category with a specific ID.

See also:

  • Db::result()

docs.title: Type Method
docs.text:
Usage: Categories::type(int $id);

Return: string

Returns the type of a specific category (e.g., 'post', 'tag').

docs.title: ID Method
docs.text:
Usage: Categories::id(string $name_or_slug);

Return: int

Returns the ID of a category based on its name or slug.

docs.title: Exist Method
docs.text:
Usage: Categories::exist(int $id);

Return: bool

Checks if a category with the specified ID exists in the database.

docs.title: Slug Method
docs.text:
Usage: Categories::slug(int $id);

Return: string

Returns the slug of a specific category.

docs.title: Comments Class
docs.text:
# filename: Comments.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 1.0.0
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Comments class manages the reader feedback system in GeniXCMS. it handles comment submission, moderation, spam protection, and threaded rendering for posts and pages.

docs.title: Form Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Comments::form();

Renders the HTML comment form. - Automatically handles user sessions (hides name/email fields for logged-in users). - Integrates Google ReCaptcha if enabled. - Includes client-side validation using BootstrapValidator.

docs.title: ShowList Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Comments::showList(['max' => 10]);

Displays a threaded list of approved comments for the current post, including pagination.

docs.title: Comment Management
docs.text:
• Comments::addComment(array $data);: Processes the $_POST data to save a new comment.
  • Comments::publish(int $id);: Approves a pending comment.
  • Comments::unpublish(int $id);: Sets a comment as unapproved (hidden).
  • Comments::delete(int $id);: Permanently removes a comment.

docs.title: Spam Protection
docs.text: The class includes several layers of defense against automated spam: 1. Bad Words Filter: The checkSpamWord() method scans content against a list of over 300 common spam phrases (maintained in spamWord()). 2. Speed Limit: The checkLastComment() method prevents users from posting multiple comments within a short timeframe (default 60 seconds). 3. Google ReCaptcha: Support for g-recaptcha-response verification via the Xaptcha class. 4. HTML Sanitization: Strictly filters allowed HTML tags using Typo::strip() to prevent XSS.

docs.title: Utilities
docs.text:
• Comments::recent(array $vars);: Returns a list of the most recent comments for use in sidebars or widgets.
  • Comments::isEnable();: returns true if the comment system is enabled in global settings.

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text: Comments are stored in the comments table with fields for: - userid / name / email / url - comment: The sanitized message body. - parent: ID of the parent comment (for threading). - status: 1 (Approved), 0 (Unapproved), 2 (Pending).

docs.title: Container Class
docs.text: The Container class is a simple Dependency Injection (DI) Container designed to decouple core services from global state and static class calls. This transition facilitates unit testing and improves overall architecture.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Available Services
  • Managing Services
  • How to Use in Controllers
  • Unit Testing with DI

docs.title: Available Services
docs.text:
GeniXCMS registers the following core services in the container during initialization:

Key Service Class Description db Db Core database controller (PDO) hooks Hooks System-wide actions and filters http Http HTTP request/response handling options Options Global configuration settings cache Cache Output and object caching token Token Security tokens for CSRF user User Authenticated user management system System Core system identity router Router URL routing logic

docs.title: Registration
docs.text:
You can register or override any service in the container at any point, although it's typically handled by the System class.

Container::set('my_service', new CustomService());

docs.title: Retrieval
docs.text:
To get a service instance stored in the container:

$db = Container::get('db');
$db->query("...");

Or use the provided static shortcuts: 

$db = Container::db();
$user = Container::user();

docs.title: How to Use in Controllers
docs.text:
All controllers extending BaseControl already have access to core services as instance properties. Avoid using static calls like Db::query() and prefer $this->db->query().

class MyControl extends BaseControl
{
    public function run($param)
    {
        // Using injected Database service
        $results = $this->db->query("SELECT * FROM table");

        // Using injected User service
        if ($this->user->access(4)) {
            // ...
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Unit Testing with DI
docs.text:
The secondary benefit of this container is the ease of mocking. In your test setup, you can inject mock objects into the controller constructor:

class MyControlTest extends \PHPUnit\Framework\TestCase
{
    public function testRun()
    {
        $mockDb = $this->createMock(Db::class);
        $mockDb->method('query')->willReturn([]);

        // Inject mock instead of using the live database
        $controller = new MyControl($mockDb);
        $controller->run([]);

        $this->assertEmpty($controller->getData('results'));
    }
}

[!TIP] While older static methods like Db::query() still work for backward compatibility, new development should exclusively use the $this->db property in controllers.

docs.title: Control Layer Reference
docs.text:
# location: inc/lib/Control/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Control Layer is the collection of individual controller files stored in inc/lib/Control/. These files are not called directly — they are loaded by the Control class dispatcher based on the current URL or admin page selection.

docs.title: Directory Structure
docs.text:
inc/lib/Control/
├── Frontend/     — Public-facing page controllers (class-based, extend BaseControl)
├── Backend/      — Admin dashboard controllers (procedural)
├── Ajax/         — JSON API / AJAX endpoint controllers
├── Error/        — Error page handlers
└── Install/      — Installation wizard controller

docs.title: Frontend Controllers
docs.text:
All frontend controllers are class-based and extend BaseControl, which provides the Latte templating engine and common data. Each file instantiates its class and calls run($param) at the bottom.

How they are called: 

// GxMain calls:
Control::frontend();
// Which calls:
Control::incFront('post', $param);
// Which includes:
inc/lib/Control/Frontend/post.control.php

File Class URL Pattern Description default.control.php DefaultControl / or /paging/N/ Homepage with paginated post listing post.control.php PostControl /slug.html Single post or page detail view page.control.php PageControl /slug.html (type=page) Static page with optional module support cat.control.php CatControl /category/ID/slug/ Category archive listing tag.control.php TagControl /tag/slug/ Tag archive listing author.control.php AuthorControl /author/username/ Author post archive archive.control.php ArchiveControl /YYYY/MM/ Date-based monthly archive search.control.php SearchControl /search/ Full-text search results login.control.php LoginControl /login/ User login form and processing register.control.php RegisterControl /register/ User self-registration with email verification forgotpass.control.php ForgotPassControl /forgotpass/ Password reset via email token logout.control.php (procedural) /logout/ Destroy session and redirect to home mod.control.php (procedural) /mod/MODULE_NAME.html Custom module frontend page loader rss.control.php (procedural) /rss/ RSS feed XML output sitemap.control.php (procedural) /sitemap.xml XML sitemap output thumb.control.php (procedural) /thumb/type/SIZE/ALIGN/file Dynamic image thumbnail via Image::thumbFly

docs.title: Standard Frontend Controller Pattern
docs.text:
class PostControl extends BaseControl
{
    public function run($param)
    {
        // 1. Extract URL parameters
        $data = Router::scrap($param);

        // 2. Fetch & prepare content
        $posts = Posts::fetch(['id' => $post_id]);
        $data['posts'] = Posts::prepare($posts);

        // 3. Trigger hooks
        $data['content'] = Hooks::run('post_content_before_action', $data);
        $data['content'] .= Posts::content($post->content);

        // 4. Render through BaseControl (header + view + footer via Latte)
        $this->render('single', $data);

        // 5. Track page view
        Stats::addViews($post_id);
    }
}

// Instantiate and run at end of file:
$control = new PostControl();
$control->run($param);

docs.title: Backend Controllers
docs.text:
Backend controllers are procedural files with no class definition. They are loaded by Control::incBack(). All operations are protected by User::access(level).

How they are called: 

// Admin URL: /admin/index.php?page=posts
Control::backend();
// Which calls:
Control::incBack('posts');
// Which includes:
inc/lib/Control/Backend/posts.control.php

File ?page= Value Min Access Level Description default.control.php (dashboard) 1 Main admin dashboard with stats posts.control.php posts 3 Blog post CRUD (add, edit, delete, bulk actions) pages.control.php pages 3 Static page CRUD categories.control.php categories 1 Post category add/edit/delete tags.control.php tags 1 Tag management comments.control.php comments 1 Comment moderation menus.control.php menus 1 Navigation menu builder modules.control.php modules 1 Module listing and install/uninstall mods.control.php mod 1 Individual module backend page loader media.control.php media 1 Media file browser (elFinder) themes.control.php themes 1 Theme listing and activation users.control.php users 1 User management (CRUD, roles) widgets.control.php widgets 1 Widget placement management settings.control.php settings 1 General site settings settings-cache.control.php settings-cache 1 File/Redis cache settings settings-comments.control.php settings-comments 1 Comment system settings settings-media.control.php settings-media 1 Image, watermark, and media settings settings-multilang.control.php settings-multilang 1 Multi-language configuration settings-permalink.control.php settings-permalink 1 Permalink / URL structure settings

docs.title: User Access Levels
docs.text: Level Role Notes 1 Administrator Full access to all backend areas 2 Editor Can manage others' content 3 Author Can only manage their own posts 4 Subscriber Frontend-only; no admin access

docs.title: Standard Backend Controller Pattern
docs.text:
if (User::access(1)) {
    // Sanitize inputs
    $name = Typo::cleanX($_POST['name']);
    $token = Typo::cleanX($_POST['token']);

    // Validate CSRF token
    if (!Token::validate($token)) {
        $alertDanger[] = _("Token not exist or expired.");
    }

    // Validate required fields
    if (empty($name)) {
        $alertDanger[] = _("Name cannot be empty.");
    }

    if (!isset($alertDanger)) {
        // Database operation
        Db::insert("INSERT INTO `table` VALUES (...)");
        $data['alertSuccess'][] = _("Saved successfully.");
        Token::remove($token);
    } else {
        $data['alertDanger'] = $alertDanger;
    }

    // Render admin template
    Theme::admin('header', $data);
    System::inc('view_name', $data);
    Theme::admin('footer');
} else {
    Theme::admin('header');
    Control::error('noaccess');
    Theme::admin('footer');
}

docs.title: Ajax Controllers
docs.text:
Ajax controllers are procedural files that return JSON responses. They are loaded by Control::ajax() when the URL matches /ajax/{endpoint}/{token}.

How they are called: 

// URL: /ajax/api/TOKEN
Control::ajax('api', $param);
// Which includes:
inc/lib/Control/Ajax/api-ajax.control.php

File Endpoint Token Required Description api-ajax.control.php api Optional (per action) REST-like CMS data API (posts, categories, tags) elfinder-ajax.control.php elfinder Yes elFinder file manager backend saveimage-ajax.control.php saveimage Yes Saves images pasted into the WYSIWYG editor tags-ajax.control.php tags Yes Tag autocomplete suggestions version-ajax.control.php version Yes Checks for latest GeniXCMS version

docs.title: Public API Endpoints (api-ajax)
docs.text:
Some actions are publicly accessible without a token:

Action (?action=) Token Parameters Returns recent_posts Not required num, type, cat Array of post objects with image, excerpt categories Not required type Array of category objects tags Required term Array of matching tag objects

Example Request: 

GET /ajax/api/TOKEN?action=recent_posts&num=5

Example Response: 

{
    "status": "success",
    "data": [
        {
            "id": 42,
            "title": "Hello World",
            "url": "https://example.com/hello-world.html",
            "image": "https://example.com/thumb/type/square/size/300/...",
            "excerpt": "Short preview..."
        }
    ]
}

docs.title: Error Controllers
docs.text:
Error controllers are simple include files at inc/lib/Control/Error/ triggered by Control::error($code).

File Code Description 404.control.php 404 Page not found noaccess.control.php noaccess Insufficient user permission unknown.control.php (default) Generic unknown error

docs.title: Control Class
docs.text:
# filename: Control.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Control class is the central dispatcher of GeniXCMS. It acts as the single entry point that reads the current URL, determines the appropriate area (frontend, backend, ajax, error, or install), and includes the corresponding controller file from the inc/lib/Control/ directory.

Important

Control does not implement business logic itself. It is purely a router/dispatcher that delegates execution to the correct controller file.

docs.title: Application Bootstrap Flow
docs.text:
index.php
  └── GxMain::frontend()
        └── Control::frontend()
              ├── Control::route()       [Smart URL mode]
              │     └── Control::incFront('post', $param)
              └── Control::get()         [Non-Smart URL mode]
                    └── Control::incFront('post')

docs.title: Frontend Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::frontend();

Main dispatcher for all public-facing pages. It determines the routing mode (Smart URL vs. query string) and delegates to either route() or get().

  • Smart URL mode: Uses Router::run() to parse the URI.
  • Non-Smart URL mode: Reads from $_GET parameters directly.

docs.title: Route Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::route(array $allowed);

Used in Smart URL mode. Calls Router::run() to match the current URI, then calls incFront() with the matched controller name and parameters. Falls back to error('404') on no match.

docs.title: Get Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::get(array $allowed);

Used in non-Smart URL mode. Iterates over $_GET parameters to find a matching controller key from the $allowed list and calls incFront().

docs.title: IncFront Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::incFront(string $controller, array $param = '');

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Frontend/{$controller}.control.php. Falls back to error('404') if the file does not exist.

// Internally called as:
Control::incFront('post', $param);
// Includes: inc/lib/Control/Frontend/post.control.php

docs.title: Backend Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::backend();

Dispatcher for the admin dashboard. Reads $_GET['page'] and includes the matching file from inc/lib/Control/Backend/. Defaults to the dashboard (default.control.php) if no page is specified.

// URL: /admin/index.php?page=posts
// Includes: inc/lib/Control/Backend/posts.control.php

docs.title: IncBack Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::incBack(string $controller);

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Backend/{$controller}.control.php. Falls back to error('404', 'File Not Found') if the file does not exist.

docs.title: Ajax Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::ajax(string $endpoint, array $param = '');

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Ajax/{$endpoint}-ajax.control.php.

// URL: /ajax/api/TOKEN
// Includes: inc/lib/Control/Ajax/api-ajax.control.php

docs.title: Error Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::error(string $code = '', mixed $val = '');

Includes a file from inc/lib/Control/Error/{$code}.control.php. Common codes: 404, noaccess.

// Show 404 page
Control::error('404');

// Show access denied page
Control::error('noaccess');

docs.title: Install Method
docs.text:
Usage: Control::install();

Includes the installation wizard controller at inc/lib/Control/Install/default.control.php.

docs.title: Allowed Frontend Routes
docs.text:
The frontend() method allows the following route keys:

['ajax', 'post', 'page', 'cat', 'mod', 'sitemap', 'rss',
 'account', 'search', 'author', 'tag', 'thumb', 'default',
 'login', 'register', 'forgotpass', 'logout', 'archive']

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• BaseControl — Abstract class extended by all frontend controllers.
  • Router — URL pattern matching, used by Control::route().
  • Control Layer Reference — Full documentation of all individual controller files.

docs.title: Date Class
docs.text:
# filename: Date.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.3
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

This Class is used to format and manage the date and timezone.

docs.title: Format Method
docs.text:
Usage: Date::format(string $date, string $format);

Return string

This method will format the date for specific format. As default it will format as j F Y H:i A T, date format according to PHP.NET Date Manual. Please read it if you want to change the date format.

docs.title: Local Method
docs.text:
Usage: Date::local(string $date, string $format);

Return string

This method will result as Local Date format according to the local timezone. The timezone is set at the backend dashboard.

docs.title: Timezone Method
docs.text:
Usage: Date::TimeZone();

Return array

This method will result the list of Timezone in array.

docs.title: optTimeZone Method
docs.text:
Usage: Date::optTimeZone();

return string

This method will result a list of timezone in an options markup.

docs.title: Country Options Dropdown
docs.text:
Usage: Date::optCountry(string $val);

return string

This method will result a list of Country in a dropdown options. If the value of the variable was set, the country which same with the variable will be selected.

docs.title: Country List Method
docs.text:
Usage: Date::countryList();

Return string

This method will result the list of country in array.

docs.title: DB Class
docs.text:
# filename: Db.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Db class is the foundational database abstraction layer for GeniXCMS. It uses PDO to handle secure, prepared-statement queries across multiple database drivers (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite).

[!IMPORTANT] Since version 2.0.0, it is highly recommended to use the Query Builder or the ORM / Model layer for standard data operations. They provide a more maintainable, expressive, and safer interface than direct Db calls.

docs.title: Connection
docs.text: Database credentials are automatically loaded from inc/config/config.php. The connection is established during system initialization.

docs.title: Query Method
docs.text:
Usage: Db::query(string $query, array $params = []);

Executes a SQL query. It is highly recommended to use the $params array for automatic escaping (prepared-statement style).

Db::query("UPDATE `users` SET `status` = '1' WHERE `id` = ?", [5]);

docs.title: Result Method
docs.text:
Usage: $data = Db::result(string $query, array $params = []);

Executes a SELECT query and returns an array of result objects (stdClass).

$users = Db::result("SELECT * FROM `user` WHERE `group` = ?", [0]);
foreach ($users as $u) {
    echo $u->userid;
}

docs.title: Insert Method
docs.text:
Usage: Db::insert(array $vars); or Db::insert(string $query);

Inserts a new record into a table.

docs.title: Array Mode (Recommended)
docs.text:
$data = array(
    'table' => 'posts',
    'key'   => array(
        'title'   => 'My Post',
        'content' => 'Hello World'
    )
);
Db::insert($data);

docs.title: Update Method
docs.text:
Usage: Db::update(array $vars); or Db::update(string $query);

Updates existing records.

docs.title: Array Mode (Recommended)
docs.text:
$data = array(
    'table' => 'posts',
    'id'    => 12, // The record ID
    'key'   => array(
        'title' => 'Updated Title'
    )
);
Db::update($data);

docs.title: Delete Method
docs.text:
Usage: Db::delete(array $vars);

Deletes records from a table.

$data = array(
    'table' => 'posts',
    'where' => array('id' => 1)
);
Db::delete($data);

docs.title: Helper Properties
docs.text:
• Db::$num_rows: Stores the number of rows affected/returned by the last query.
  • Db::$last_id: Stores the ID generated by the last INSERT query.

docs.title: Escape Method
docs.text:
Usage: Db::escape(string $string);

Returns a string safe for SQL insertion. Note: Manual escaping is unnecessary when using the $params argument in query() or result().

docs.title: Files Class
docs.text:
# filename: Files.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.2
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Files class provides utilities for file system management, security scanning of uploaded files, and integration with the elFinder file manager.

docs.title: DelTree Method
docs.text:
Usage: Files::delTree(string $directory);

Recursively deletes a directory and all of its contents (files and subdirectories).

Files::delTree('path/to/old_module');

docs.title: IsClean Method
docs.text:
Usage: Files::isClean(string $file_path);

Return: bool

Scans a file for potentially malicious code patterns. It checks both local and remote files for: - PHP execution keywords (eval, base64_decode, system, etc.). - Embedded scripts (<script>, javascript:, etc.). - Suspicious HTML entities and encoded characters.

docs.title: IsRemote / RemoteExist Methods
docs.text:
• Files::isRemote(string $path);: Checks if a path is a remote URL.
  • Files::remoteExist(string $url);: Uses CURL to check if a remote file is reachable (HTTP 200).

docs.title: ElfinderLib Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Files::elfinderLib();

Returns the required HTML, CSS, and JavaScript to initialize the elFinder file manager. This is used in the admin area to provide a graphical interface for managing site assets.

It includes: - UI compatibility shims. - JavaScript handlers for file selection. - Callback functions for integrating with the Summernote editor (elfinderDialog) and featured image selection (elfinderDialog2).

docs.title: GxMain Class
docs.text:
# filename: GxMain.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The GxMain class is the secondary entry point of the application, responsible for directing traffic to the appropriate controllers based on the system state (installed vs. not installed) and the requested area (frontend vs. backend).

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text: The constructor checks for the existence of the configuration file: - If inc/config/config.php exists, it initializes the System class. - If not, it triggers the install() method.

docs.title: Index Method
docs.text:
Usage: $gx = new GxMain(); $gx->index();

Loads the default frontend controller. This is typically called from the root index.php.

docs.title: Admin Method
docs.text:
Usage: $gx = new GxMain(); $gx->admin();

Handles access to the administrative dashboard: 1. Secures the session (requires login). 2. Checks user permissions (requires level 4 or better). 3. Loads the backend controller or an error page if access is denied.

docs.title: Install Method
docs.text:
Usage: $gx = new GxMain(); $gx->install();

Initializes the installation process by loading the installation theme and controller.

docs.title: Hooks Class
docs.text:
# filename: Hooks.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.6
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Hooks class implements the event-driven architecture of GeniXCMS. It allows modules and themes to "hook" into system processes by attaching functions to specific execution points (actions) or by modifying data (filters).

docs.title: Core Concepts
docs.text:
• Actions (run): Execution points where you can perform a task (e.g., sending an email after post submission).
  • Filters (filter): Points where you can modify a piece of data before it is processed or displayed (e.g., modifying the post content).

docs.title: Attach Method
docs.text:
Usage: Hooks::attach(string $hook_name, callable $function);

Registers a function to be executed when a specific hook is triggered.

Hooks::attach('footer_load_lib', 'my_custom_script_loader');

docs.title: Run Method (Actions)
docs.text:
Usage: Hooks::run(string $hook_name, $args...);

Triggers all functions attached to an action hook. Multiple arguments can be passed to the attached functions.

// Inside core system
Hooks::run('post_delete_action', $post_id);

docs.title: Filter Method (Filters)
docs.text:
Usage: $data = Hooks::filter(string $hook_name, $data, $args...);

Passes data through all functions attached to a filter hook. Each function must return the modified data.

// Inside core system
$title = Hooks::filter('post_title_filter', $title);

docs.title: Available Hooks
docs.text:
GeniXCMS provides dozens of built-in hooks, including:

  • init: System initialization.
  • header_load_meta: Loading meta tags in the header.
  • footer_load_lib: Loading JavaScript libraries in the footer.
  • post_content_filter: Filtering post body content.
  • post_submit_add_action: Triggered after a new post is saved to DB.
  • admin_page_top_action: Content at the top of an admin page.
  • user_login_action: Triggered after a successful user login.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Naming: Use unique prefixes for your hook functions to avoid collisions with other modules.
  2. Arguments: Attached functions receive arguments as an array.
  3. Return Values: Filter functions MUST return the expected data type, otherwise the system data may be corrupted.

docs.title: Http Class
docs.text:
# filename: Http.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 1.0.0
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Http class provides utilities for handling HTTP requests, URL validation, and IP-based geolocation. It also includes methods for extracting authentication headers.

docs.title: URL Validation
docs.text:
• Http::validateUrl(string $url);: Checks if a URL is valid, uses allowed protocols, and respects local/remote security rules.
  • Http::validateProtocol(string $url, array $protocols);: Verifies the URI scheme (e.g., http, https).
  • Http::validatePort(string $url, array $ports);: Checks if the URL uses an allowed port.
  • Http::isLocal(string $url);: Returns true if the URL points to a local network address (e.g., 127.0.0.1, 192.168.x.x).

docs.title: Fetch Method
docs.text:
Usage: Http::fetch($vars);

A versatile method to fetch content from a URL. It can use either file_get_contents() or CURL depending on the configuration.

$vars = [
    'url'          => 'https://example.com/api',
    'curl'         => true,
    'curl_options' => [CURLOPT_TIMEOUT => 30]
];
$data = Http::fetch($vars);

docs.title: IP & Geolocation
docs.text:
• Http::ipDetail(string $ip);: Fetches detailed JSON information about an IP address using external geolocation APIs.
  • Http::getIpCountry(string $ip);: Returns the 2-letter country code for a specific IP.

docs.title: Authorization
docs.text:
• Http::getAuthorizationHeader();: Extracts the Authorization header from the request, handling various server environments (Apache, Nginx, CGI).
  • Http::getBearerToken();: Specifically extracts a Bearer token from the authorization header, useful for REST API authentication.

docs.title: User Agent Utilities
docs.text: The class maintains a list of common browser user agents, which can be used to mask automated requests: - Http::randAgent();: Returns a random user agent string. - Http::addAgent($agent);: Adds a custom agent to the internal list.

docs.title: Image Class
docs.text:
# filename: Image.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License
# requires: Intervention\Image v3 (GD Driver), php-gd, php-imagick (optional)

The Image class is the primary image processing engine for GeniXCMS. It provides on-the-fly thumbnail generation with caching, image resizing and cropping, format conversion (WebP), compression utilities, and Gravatar support.

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
• Intervention\Image v3 (via Composer, GD Driver): Used for thumbFly and convertWebp.
  • PHP GD Extension: Required for resize, compressPng, and thumbFly2.
  • PHP Imagick Extension (optional): Used by compressJpg if available.

docs.title: ThumbFly Method (Recommended)
docs.text:
Usage: Image::thumbFly(string $img, string $type, int $size, string $align);

The primary, modern thumbnail generator powered by Intervention Image v3. It generates and caches thumbnails on the first request, then serves the cached file on subsequent requests without re-processing.

  • $img: Relative or remote URL to the source image.
  • $type: Resize mode — 'square', 'large', or 'small'.
  • $size: The target dimension in pixels. Defaults: square=150, large=200, small=100.
  • $align: Alignment for crop operations (not used directly in thumbFly).

Resize Modes:

Type Method Default square cover() — crops to exact square 150px large scale(width) — scales proportionally 200px small scale(width) — scales proportionally 100px

Cache Location: assets/cache/thumbs/thumb{type}{size}{align}-{hash}.{ext}

Security: Before processing, the file is validated using Files::isClean() to reject remote files containing malicious code.

Watermark Support: If media_use_watermark is enabled in settings and the image is larger than 200px, a watermark image is automatically overlaid using Intervention\Image::place().

Output Optimization: - JPEG/WebP: Progressive encoding at quality 65. - AVIF/WebP: Quality 65.

docs.title: ThumbFly2 Method (Legacy)
docs.text:
Usage: Image::thumbFly2(string $img, string $type, int $size, string $align);

An older, GD-based thumbnail generator. It handles square, large, and small types with manual alignment logic (top, bottom, left, right, center). For new implementations, prefer thumbFly.

docs.title: Resize Method
docs.text:
Usage: Image::resize(string $src, string $dst, int $width, int $height, bool $crop = false);

A native GD-based resize function. Supports BMP, GIF, JPG, PNG and preserves transparency for GIF/PNG.

  • $crop = 0: Scales proportionally to fit within the given dimensions.
  • $crop = 1: Crops to exact dimensions (center-crop).
Image::resize(
    GX_PATH . '/assets/uploads/photo.jpg',
    GX_PATH . '/assets/cache/photo-thumb.jpg',
    300,
    200,
    1 // crop
);

docs.title: CompressPng Method
docs.text:
Usage: Image::compressPng(string $path);

Recompresses a PNG file using GD at compression level 9 while preserving alpha channel transparency.

docs.title: CompressPng2 Method
docs.text:
Usage: Image::compressPng2(string $path, int $max_quality = 80);

Uses the pngquant command-line tool (if installed on the server) to perform lossy PNG compression between 70–80% quality. Returns false if pngquant is unavailable.

docs.title: CompressJpg Method
docs.text:
Usage: Image::compressJpg(string $path, int $quality = 80);

Uses the Imagick PHP extension (if installed) to re-compress a JPEG file and strip EXIF metadata.

docs.title: ConvertWebp Method
docs.text:
Usage: Image::convertWebp(string $img);

Converts a local image to WebP format using Intervention Image v3, saving the output as {original_filename}.webp at quality 65. Browsers can then be served the smaller WebP version.

docs.title: GetGravatar Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Image::getGravatar(string $email, int $size = 60);

Returns the Gravatar URL (or <img> tag) for a user's email address. Falls back to a local default image if the site is in offline mode.

// Return URL only
$url = Image::getGravatar('user@example.com', 80);

// Return full <img> tag
$img = Image::getGravatar('user@example.com', 80, 'mm', 'g', true, ['class' => 'avatar']);

docs.title: IsJpg / IsPng Methods
docs.text:
• Image::isJpg($file);: Returns true if the file extension is jpg.
  • Image::isPng($file);: Returns true if the file extension is png.

docs.title: URL Format (Smart Thumbnails)
docs.text:
Thumbnails are accessed via a special URL pattern routed by the Router class:

# Square thumbnail, 300px
/thumb/type/square/size/300/image-path.jpg

# Large thumbnail with alignment  
/thumb/type/large/size/400/align/top/image-path.jpg

# Simple size-only
/thumb/size/150/image-path.jpg

The Mail class provides a wrapper for PHPMailer, allowing the system to send emails via standard PHP mail() or through an external SMTP server.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Settings for the Mail class are managed in the admin dashboard (System Settings): - smtphost: SMTP Server address. - smtpuser: SMTP Username. - smtppass: SMTP Password. - smtpport: SMTP Port (25, 465, 587). - mailtype: 0 for PHP Mail, 1 for SMTP.

docs.title: Send Method
docs.text:
Usage: Mail::send(array $vars);

Sends an email using the pre-configured method.

$vars = array(
    'to'      => 'recipient@example.com',
    'to_name' => 'John Doe',
    'subject' => 'Test Email',
    'message' => '<h1>Hello!</h1><p>This is a test email.</p>',
    'msgtype' => 'html' // Optional: 'html' (default) or 'text'
);

$res = Mail::send($vars);
if ($res !== true) {
    echo "Error: " . $res; // Returns error message string on failure
}

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text:
1. Loads SMTP/Site settings from Options.
  2. Instantiates PHPMailer.
  3. Handles exceptions and returns error messages if the send fails.
  4. Supports both HTML and Plain Text content types.

The Menus class handles the creation, management, and rendering of navigation menus. It supports up to 4 levels of nested submenus and integrates with the GeniXCMS URL system.

docs.title: GetMenu Method (Frontend)
docs.text:
Usage: echo Menus::getMenu(string $menuid, string $class = '', bool $bsnav = false);

Generates HTML for a specific menu. - $menuid: The unique identifier of the menu (e.g., 'mainmenu'). - $class: Custom CSS class for the root <ul>. - $bsnav: If set to true, it generates Bootstrap 5 compatible dropdown markup.

echo Menus::getMenu('top-menu', 'navbar-nav ms-auto', true);

docs.title: GetMenuAdmin Method (Backend)
docs.text:
Usage: echo Menus::getMenuAdmin(string $menuid);

Generates an interactive management interface for a menu, including reordering and editing capabilities, used within the admin dashboard.

docs.title: Management Methods
docs.text:
Methods for CRUD operations on menu items:

  • Menus::insert(array $vars);
  • Menus::update(array $vars);
  • Menus::delete(int $id);
  • Menus::updateMenuOrder(array $vars);

docs.title: Data Retrieval
docs.text:
• Menus::getMenuRaw(string $menuid);: Returns an array of all menu items (as objects) for a specific menu ID.
  • Menus::id(int $id);: Returns a specific menu item object.
  • Menus::isHadParent(int $parent, string $menuid);: Checks for children of a specific parent node.

docs.title: Internal Logic
docs.text: The class uses a recursive-like approach (hardcoded up to 4 levels) to build nested lists. Each menu item can be of various types (post, page, category, custom), and the class automatically uses Url::type($value) to generate the correct destination link.

docs.title: Migration System Documentation
docs.text: GeniXCMS features a command-line driven migration system that allows you to manage database schema changes programmatically. This ensures that all environments (development, staging, production) are running on the same version of the database.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Overview
  • How to Run Migrations
  • Creating a Migration
  • Method Reference

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Migrations are PHP files stored in inc/migrations/. Each file contains a class that extends Migration and implements two methods: - up(): Performed when running migrations (adding table, column, etc.) - down(): Performed when rolling back migrations (dropping table, column, etc.)

docs.title: How to Run Migrations
docs.text: GeniXCMS includes a CLI tool called gx-console located in the root of your installation.

docs.title: 🚀 Running Pending Migrations
docs.text:
To execute all new migrations that have not yet been run:

php gx-console migrate

docs.title: ⏪ Rolling Back Last Migration
docs.text:
If you need to undo the most recent batch of migrations:

php gx-console migrate:rollback

docs.title: Creating a Migration
docs.text: Migrations must follow a specific naming convention: - Filename: YYYYMMDD-batchid-SomeDescription.php - (Example: 20240327-01-CreatePermissionsTable.php) - Class Name: Migration_YYYYMMDD_batchid_SomeDescription - (Example: class Migration_20240327_01_CreatePermissionsTable extends Migration)

docs.title: Example Migration File
docs.text:
<?php

class Migration_20240327_10_AddProductTable extends Migration
{
    public function up()
    {
        $sql = "CREATE TABLE `products` (
            `id` int(11) NOT NULL PRIMARY KEY AUTO_INCREMENT,
            `name` varchar(255) NOT NULL,
            `price` decimal(10,2) NOT NULL
        ) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8;";
        Db::query($sql);
    }

    public function down()
    {
        Db::query("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS `products` ");
    }
}

docs.title: Migration::init()
docs.text: Used internally to ensure the migrations table exists in the database.

docs.title: Migration::run()
docs.text: Executed via CLI to find and run all pending migrations in alphabetical order.

docs.title: Migration::rollback()
docs.text:
Executed via CLI to find the last batch of migrations and run their down() methods.

[!TIP] Always implement the down() method correctly so that you can safely roll back changes if something goes wrong during deployment!

$data = Mod::data('my-module');
echo $data['name']; // Module Name
echo $data['version']; // 1.0.0

docs.title: Model Class Documentation (ORM)
docs.text: The Model class provides a simple ActiveRecord-style ORM for interacting with database rows as PHP objects. By extending the Model class, your domain entities can inherit complex data manipulation features with minimal code.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Creating a Model
  • Finding Records
  • Saving & Updating
  • Deleting Records
  • Model Chaining

docs.title: Creating a Model
docs.text:
To create a new model, simply extend the Model class and define your table name.

abstract class Post extends Model 
{
    protected $table = 'posts';
    protected $primaryKey = 'id'; // Default is 'id'
}

docs.title: static Model::find($id)
docs.text:
Retrieve a single record by its primary key.

$post = Posts::find(10);
if ($post) {
    echo $post->title;
}

docs.title: static Model::all()
docs.text:
Retrieve all records from the table for this model.

$all_posts = Posts::all();
foreach ($all_posts as $post) {
    echo $post->title;
}

docs.title: Creating New Data
docs.text:
You can instantiate the model, set attributes, and call save().

$post = new Posts();
$post->title = "Brand New Post";
$post->status = "1";
$post->save();

// Or use the static create method
Posts::create(['title' => 'Fast Creation', 'status' => '1']);

docs.title: Updating Data
docs.text:
Retrieve an existing model, modify it, and call save().

$post = Posts::find(5);
$post->title = "Updated Title";
$post->save();

docs.title: delete()
docs.text:
Remove the current model instance from the database.

$post = Posts::find(12);
$post->delete();

docs.title: Model Chaining (Active Record + Query Builder)
docs.text:
Models integrate natively with the Query Builder, allowing you to start a query directly from your class.

// Find specific records
$posts = Posts::where('status', '1')->orderBy('date', 'desc')->get();

// Complex filtering
$authors = Posts::where('author', 'admin')
    ->where('date', '>', '2023-01-01')
    ->get();

docs.title: Method Summary
docs.text: Method Description all() Retrieve all records find($id) Look up record by ID where($col, $op, $val) Start a query with a condition create($data) Quick insert save() Save/Update current object delete() Delete current object

docs.title: Dynamic Attributes
docs.text:
The Model class uses PHP's magic methods (__get and __set) to allow accessing database columns as if they were properties of the object.

echo $post->userid; // Accesses 'userid' from the attributes array

[!IMPORTANT] When using Model::query(), the returned objects are instances of the target model, allowing you to manipulate them and call save() or delete() directly.

The Options class manages site-wide settings stored in the database. These options control everything from site name to plugin configurations.

docs.title: Insert Method
docs.text:
Usage: Options::insert(array $vars);

Inserts multiple options at once.

$vars = array(
    'my_setting' => 'value1',
    'another_setting' => 'value2'
);
Options::insert($vars);

docs.title: Update Method
docs.text:
Usage: Options::update($key, $val = '');

Updates an existing option or a set of options.

// Single update
Options::update('site_name', 'My New Site');

// Multiple updates
$data = array('site_name' => 'Site A', 'site_email' => 'a@site.com');
Options::update($data);

docs.title: Get Method

Retrieves an option directly from the database.

docs.title: V Method (Value)
docs.text:
Usage: echo Options::v(string $name);

echo Options::v('site_name');

Usage: Options::validate(string $name); Usage: Options::isExist(string $name);

Checks if an option exists in the database.

docs.title: OptionsBuilder Class
docs.text: The OptionsBuilder class is a high-level UI utility in GeniXCMS designed to build professional administration settings panels for themes and modules with minimal effort. It generates a tabbed interface, handles field rendering, and includes a dynamic frontend CSS generator.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Tabbed Interface: Group settings into logical tabs and sidebar groups.
  • Rich Fields: Support for color pickers, range sliders, toggles, and typography groups.
  • Frontend CSS: Automatically generates <style> blocks based on saved options, including Google Fonts integration.
  • Responsive Grid: Built-in support for 2 and 3 column field layouts.

docs.title: Basic Usage (Admin Panel)
docs.text:
To use the builder in your theme's options.php or a module's settings:

// 1. Prepare your saved data
$opt = json_decode(Options::get('my_theme_options'), true) ?? [];

// 2. Define the UI Schema
$schema = [
    [
        'id'       => 'tab-general',
        'label'    => 'General',
        'icon'     => 'fa fa-cog',
        'group'    => 'Settings',
        'title'    => 'General Settings',
        'cards'    => [
            [
                'title'  => 'Hero Content',
                'fields' => [
                    ['type' => 'text', 'name' => 'hero_title', 'label' => 'Title'],
                    ['type' => 'color', 'name' => 'primary_color', 'label' => 'Accent Color'],
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

// 3. Render the panel
$builder = new OptionsBuilder($opt, [], [], [
    'brandName' => 'My Awesome Theme',
    'saveKey'   => 'my_theme_save_btn'
]);
$builder->render($schema);

docs.title: Field Types
docs.text: Type Description text Standard text input. textarea Multi-line text area. number Numeric input with min/max. color Advanced color picker with hex input. range Slider with live value badge. select Dropdown menu. toggle Elegant On/Off switch. typo_row Composite field (Font, Size, Weight, Color). divider Horizontal separator. heading Subsection label.

docs.title: Frontend CSS Generation
docs.text:
The builder can generate a complete <style> block for your theme's frontend, including automatic Google Font loading.

// In your theme's function.php
public static function loadCSS() {
    $opt = json_decode(Options::get('my_theme_options'), true);

    return OptionsBuilder::generateFrontendCSS($opt, [
        'themeUrl' => Url::theme(),
        'minify'   => true
    ]);
}

docs.title: __construct(array $opt, array $presets = [], array $palettes = [], array $config = [])
docs.text: Initializes a new builder instance.

docs.title: render(array $schema)
docs.text: Outputs the full HTML for the administrator settings panel.

docs.title: static generateFrontendCSS(array $opt, array $config = [])
docs.text: Returns a string containing <link> tags for Google Fonts and a <style> block.

docs.title: Pages Class
docs.text:
# filename: Pages.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 1.0.0
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Pages class provides a set of conditional helper methods used within themes to identify the current content type or page being viewed. These methods wrap common checks against the global $data variable.

docs.title: Page Type Checks
docs.text:
The following methods return true if the current request matches the specified type:

  • Pages::isPage();: Current view is a static page.
  • Pages::isPost();: Current view is a blog post.
  • Pages::isCat();: Current view is a category archive listing.
  • Pages::isTag();: Current view is a tag archive listing.
  • Pages::isHome();: Current view is the site index (homepage).
  • Pages::isMod();: Current view is a custom module page.

docs.title: Use Case Example
docs.text:
These methods are essential for theme logic, particularly when deciding whether to display elements like breadcrumbs, sidebars, or meta-data.

// In a theme file (e.g., sidebar.php)
if (Pages::isPost()) {
    // Show related posts or author bio only on post pages
    Theme::inc('sidebar-blog');
} elseif (Pages::isPage()) {
    // Show a different sidebar for static pages
    Theme::inc('sidebar-page');
}

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text: The methods rely on the p_type key within the global $data array, which is populated by the Control class after routing is completed.

docs.title: Paging Class
docs.text:
# filename: Paging.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Paging class generates HTML pagination links for both administrative data tables and frontend content lists. It supports numbered pagination and simple "Previous/Next" pagers.

docs.title: Create Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Paging::create(array $vars, bool $smart = false);

Generates the pagination HTML. - $vars: An associative array containing configuration settings. - $smart: If true, it generates SEO-friendly URLs (e.g., /paging/2/) instead of query parameters (e.g., &paging=2).

docs.title: Configuration Options ($vars)
docs.text: Key Description table The database table name to calculate total records from. where (Optional) SQL WHERE clause for counting total records. max The maximum number of items displayed per page. url The base URL for the pagination links. paging The current page number. type 'number' (numbered list) or 'pager' (Prev/Next). total (Optional) Manually specify the total record count.

docs.title: Example: Numbered Pagination
docs.text:
$vars = array(
    'table' => 'posts',
    'where' => "type='post' AND status='1'",
    'max'   => 10,
    'url'   => Site::$url . 'blog/',
    'paging'=> 1,
    'type'  => 'number'
);

echo Paging::create($vars, true);

docs.title: Example: Simple Pager
docs.text:
$vars = array(
    'table' => 'user',
    'max'   => 20,
    'url'   => 'index.php?page=users',
    'paging'=> 1,
    'type'  => 'pager'
);

echo Paging::create($vars);

docs.title: Internal Logic
docs.text: The class automatically calculates the required number of pages by querying the database using the provided table and where parameters. It then builds a Bootstrap-compatible <ul> list with the appropriate active/inactive states for each page button.

docs.title: Params Class
docs.text:
# filename: Params.class.php

The Params class provides a framework for extending the "Post" and "Page" editor screens in the administrative dashboard with custom input fields. These custom fields are automatically saved and retrieved as meta-data for the content.

docs.title: Registering Custom Fields
docs.text:
Modules can register groups of fields to be displayed in either the sidebar or the bottom of the editor form.

Usage: Params::register(array $config);

docs.title: Configuration Structure
docs.text:
$my_params = [
    'sidebar' => [ // or 'bottom'
        [
            'groupname'  => 'product_meta',
            'grouptitle' => 'Product Details',
            'fields'     => [
                [
                    'name'     => 'product_price',
                    'title'    => 'Price',
                    'type'     => 'text',
                    'boxclass' => 'col-md-12'
                ],
                [
                    'name'     => 'product_color',
                    'title'    => 'Color',
                    'type'     => 'dropdown',
                    'value'    => ['Red', 'Blue', 'Green'],
                    'boxclass' => 'col-md-12'
                ]
            ]
        ]
    ]
];

Params::register($my_params);

docs.title: Field Types Supported
docs.text:
• text: Standard input field.
  • checkbox: Single checkbox.
  • textarea: Multi-line text area.
  • dropdown: Select menu (requires value array).

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text:
1. Rendering: The class attaches itself to the post_param_form_bottom and post_param_form_sidebar hooks.
  2. Data Handling: When the editor page loads, Params automatically checks if a value already exists for each field using Posts::getParam().
  3. Saving: The field names are prefixed with param[...]. The core Posts class handles the extraction and saving of these parameters during the post-save process.

docs.title: Utility Methods
docs.text:
• Params::renderSidebar();: Manually triggers the rendering of all registered sidebar parameter groups.
  • Params::renderBottom();: Manually triggers the rendering of all registered bottom parameter groups.

docs.title: Pinger Class
docs.text:
# filename: Pinger.class.php

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Pinger class notify search engines and directory services via Weblog XML-RPC ping when new content is published or updated on your site.

docs.title: Run Method
docs.text:
Usage: Pinger::run($services);

Executes the ping process for multiple services. - $services: Can be an array of service URLs or a multi-line string with one URL per line.

$services = array('rpc.pingomatic.com', 'blogsearch.google.com/ping/RPC2');
Pinger::run($services);

docs.title: IsOn Method
docs.text:
Usage: Pinger::isOn();

Return: bool

Checks if the pinger functionality is enabled in the system settings.

docs.title: Rpc Method (Internal)
docs.text:
Usage: Pinger::rpc(string $url);

The internal method that performs the actual XML-RPC call. It attempts an extendedPing first and falls back to a basic ping if necessary.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: The class requires the IXR Library (Incubator XML-RPC) to handle the network communication and XML formatting.

docs.title: Posts Class
