Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Posts class is the primary interface for managing all content types (posts, pages, etc.) in GeniXCMS.

docs.title: Insert Method
docs.text:
Usage: Posts::insert(array $vars);

This method inserts a new post into the database. It automatically generates a unique slug based on the title.

$vars = array(
    'title'   => 'My New Post',
    'cat'     => 1,
    'content' => 'Hello World!',
    'author'  => 'admin',
    'type'    => 'post',
    'status'  => '1',
    'date'    => date('Y-m-d H:i:s')
);
Posts::insert($vars);

docs.title: Update Method
docs.text:
Usage: Posts::update(array $vars);

Updates an existing post. The ID is normally retrieved from $_GET['id'].

$vars = array(
    'title'   => 'Updated Title',
    'content' => 'Updated content here...'
);
Posts::update($vars);

docs.title: Delete Method
docs.text:
Usage: Posts::delete(int $id);

Deletes a post and its associated parameters and comments.

docs.title: Content Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Posts::content(string $content);

Processes post content, handling the [[--readmore--]] tag and applying filters.

docs.title: Format Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Posts::format(string $content, int $id);

Processes content for archive/list views, truncating at the [[--readmore--]] tag and adding a "Read More" link.

docs.title: Recent Method
docs.text:
Usage: Posts::recent(array $vars);

Retrieves a list of recent posts based on criteria.

$vars = array(
    'num'  => 5,
    'type' => 'post',
    'cat'  => 1 // Optional
);
$posts = Posts::recent($vars);

docs.title: Title Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Posts::title(int $id);

Returns the title of a specific post.

docs.title: GetParam / AddParam / EditParam
docs.text:
Methods for managing post metadata (parameters).

  • Posts::addParam($param, $value, $post_id);
  • Posts::editParam($param, $value, $post_id);
  • echo Posts::getParam($param, $post_id);
  • Posts::delParam($param, $post_id);
  • Posts::existParam($param, $post_id);

docs.title: GetPostImage
docs.text:
Usage: echo Posts::getPostImage(int $post_id);

Retrieves the featured image path for a post.

docs.title: GetImage
docs.text:
Usage: echo Posts::getImage(string $content, int $number = 1);

Extracts the Nth image URL from the post content.

docs.title: Query Builder Documentation
docs.text: The Query class provides a fluent, chainable interface for building and executing database queries in GeniXCMS. It improves code readability while automatically handling security via PDO prepared statements.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Basic Usage
  • Selecting Data
  • Where Clauses
  • Joins
  • Ordering & Pagination
  • Inserts, Updates, & Deletes

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
The entry point is the static table() method. It returns a new Query instance targeted at that table.

$query = Query::table('posts');

docs.title: select($columns = '*')
docs.text:
Define which columns to retrieve. Strings or arrays are accepted.

// Single column
$titles = Query::table('posts')->select('title')->get();

// Multiple columns (array)
$data = Query::table('posts')->select(['id', 'title', 'content'])->get();

docs.title: where($column, $operator, $value)
docs.text:
Adds a basic WHERE condition. If no operator is provided, = is assumed.

// Basic equality
$active = Query::table('posts')->where('status', '1')->get();

// Custom operators
$recent = Query::table('posts')->where('date', '>', '2024-01-01')->get();

docs.title: orWhere($column, $operator, $value)
docs.text:
Combines conditions using OR.

$posts = Query::table('posts')
    ->where('type', 'post')
    ->orWhere('type', 'page')
    ->get();

docs.title: join($table, $first, $operator, $second, $type = 'INNER')
docs.text:
Joint another table into the query.

$results = Query::table('posts')
    ->select('posts.*, user.userid as author_name')
    ->join('user', 'posts.author', '=', 'user.userid')
    ->get();

docs.title: orderBy($column, $direction = 'ASC')
docs.text:
Sets the sorting order.

$latest = Query::table('posts')->orderBy('date', 'DESC')->get();

docs.title: limit($limit, $offset = null)
docs.text:
Constraints the number of results and optionally sets an offset.

$paged = Query::table('posts')->limit(10, 20)->get();

docs.title: insert($data)
docs.text:
Inserts a new row into the table.

Query::table('posts')->insert([
    'title' => 'My New Post',
    'status' => '1',
    'date' => date('Y-m-d H:i:s')
]);

docs.title: update($data)
docs.text:
Updates existing rows matching the where conditions.

Query::table('posts')
    ->where('id', 5)
    ->update(['status' => '0']);

docs.title: delete()
docs.text:
Deletes rows matching the where conditions.

Query::table('posts')->where('status', '0')->delete();

docs.title: Execution Methods
docs.text:
• get(): Returns an array of objects (all matches).
  • first(): Returns a single object (first match) or null.
  • count(): Returns the total number of records matching the criteria.

[!TIP] Use Query for complex data retrieval that involves multiple tables. For simple row/object interactions, consider using the Model layer.

docs.title: RESTful API Documentation
docs.text: GeniXCMS 2.0.0-alpha introduces a built-in RESTful API engine, allowing you to use the CMS as a Headless content provider. The API uses JSON for both requests and responses.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Authentication
  • Endpoint Structure
  • Standard Resources
  • Posts
  • Response Formats

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
The API requires a valid API Key for most operations. You can provide the key in two ways:

  1. Header: GX-API-KEY: your_api_key_here
  2. Query Parameter: ?api_key=your_api_key_here

[!NOTE] If you are accessing the API from the same browser where you are logged into the Admin Dashboard, the session will be used as a fallback authentication.

docs.title: Endpoint Structure
docs.text: The base URL for all API requests is: https://yourdomain.com/api/v1/{resource}/{identifier}

docs.title: Posts
docs.text: Action Method URL List Posts GET /api/v1/posts/ Get Single Post GET /api/v1/posts/{id} Create Post POST /api/v1/posts/ Update Post PUT /api/v1/posts/{id} Delete Post DELETE /api/v1/posts/{id}

docs.title: Create/Update Post Example (Body)
docs.text:
{
    "title": "My API Post",
    "content": "Content created via REST API",
    "status": "1",
    "type": "post"
}

docs.title: Success Response (200 OK)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "success",
    "message": "Operation successful",
    "data": { ... }
}

docs.title: Error Response (4xx / 5xx)
docs.text:
{
    "status": "error",
    "code": 404,
    "message": "Resource 'posts' not found"
}

docs.title: Technical Details
docs.text:
• Namespace: api/v1/
  • Dispatcher: Api::dispatch()
  • Controller Location: inc/lib/Control/Api/{Resource}Api.class.php
  • Autoloading: Shared with core classes in inc/lib/.

[!TIP] You can create your own API resources by adding a new class in inc/lib/Control/Api/ following the naming convention ResourceNameApi. The engine will automatically route requests based on the URL.

docs.title: Router Class
docs.text:
# filename: Router.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.7
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Router class is responsible for parsing "Smart URLs" (Permalinks) and mapping them to specific system controllers and parameters. It supports regex-based routing and can be extended by modules.

docs.title: Routing Map
docs.text:
The router uses a predefined map of URI patterns to parameters. Examples include:

  • category/([0-9]+)/(.*)/ => cat=ID
  • tag/(.*)/ => tag=SLUG
  • ([a-z]{2})/mod/(.*).html => mod=MODULE_NAME, lang=XX
  • rss/ => rss

docs.title: Run Method
docs.text:
Usage: $params = Router::run();

The main execution points of the router. It: 1. Detects the current requested URI (handling subfolder installations). 2. Matches the URI against the routing map. 3. Extracts and returns an array of parameters if a match is found.

docs.title: Add Method
docs.text:
Usage: Router::add(array $new_routes);

Allows modules to register custom routing rules. New routes are merged with the existing map and sorted by length to ensure precise matches.

$my_route = ['product/([0-9]+)/' => ['product' => 1]];
Router::add($my_route);

docs.title: Scrap Method
docs.text:
Usage: $data = Router::scrap(array $params);

Converts the internal router parameter format into a flat associative array suitable for use in controllers.

docs.title: URL Handling Features
docs.text:
1. Subfolder Support: The router automatically detects if GeniXCMS is installed in a subdirectory and strips the folder name from the URI before matching.
  2. Permalink Flexibility: Supports both "Clean URLs" (via .htaccess) and "index.php" based permalinks if mod_rewrite is unavailable.
  3. Locale Support: Automatically detects 2-letter language codes in the URL (e.g., /en/my-post.html) and passes them as a lang parameter.

docs.title: Global Prefix
docs.text: The router respects the GX_URL_PREFIX constant (usually .html), ensuring that generated links and routes remain consistent across the application.

docs.title: Rss Class
docs.text:
# filename: Rss.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS

# license: MIT License

Info

The Rss class generates XML-compliant RSS 2.0 feeds for the website's posts or other content types.

docs.title: Create Method
docs.text:
Usage: Rss::create(int $count = 20, string $url_method = 'post', string $type = 'post');

Generates and outputs the RSS feed directly to the browser. - $count: Maximum number of items in the XML feed. - $url_method: The method of the Url class to use for generating item links. - $type: The post type to include in the feed.

// Standard feed for the latest 20 posts
Rss::create();

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text:
1. Fetches recent content using Posts::recent().
  2. Sets the Content-Type: text/xml header.
  3. Builds the XML structure with appropriate channel metadata from Site properties.
  4. Iterates through items, stripping shortcodes like [[--readmore--]] and cleaning up the content string for XML compatibility.

docs.title: Security Infrastructure Documentation
docs.text: GeniXCMS implements modern web security practices at the core level to protect both the administration dashboard and front-end themes from common vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Table of Contents
docs.text:
• Content Security Policy (CSP)
  • Hardenning Headers
  • How to Extend CSP
  • CSRF Protection

docs.title: Content Security Policy (CSP)
docs.text: CSP is an added layer of security that helps to detect and mitigate certain types of attacks, including Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) and data injection attacks. GeniXCMS sends a default CSP header on every request.

docs.title: Default Policy Directives
docs.text:
• default-src: 'self' (Only allow resources from our own domain by default).
  • script-src: Allows self, 'unsafe-inline', 'unsafe-eval' (required by some legacy components like elFinder), and trusted CDNs (cdn.jsdelivr.net, code.jquery.com, cdnjs.cloudflare.com).
  • style-src: Allows self, inline styles, and trusted CDNs.
  • img-src: Allows self, data URIs, and any HTTPS source (*).
  • font-src: Allows self and trusted font providers (fonts.gstatic.com).
  • object-src: 'none' (Disallows plugins like Flash).

docs.title: Hardenning Headers
docs.text:
In addition to CSP, GeniXCMS automatically sends the following security-focused headers:

  • X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff: Prevents the browser from "sniffing" the MIME type, forcing it to stick to the declared Content-Type.
  • X-Frame-Options: SAMEORIGIN: Prevents the site from being embedded in an <iframe> on external websites, mitigating Clickjacking.
  • X-XSS-Protection: 1; mode=block: Enables the XSS filter built into many modern and legacy browsers.
  • Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin: Protects user privacy by limiting the amount of referrer data sent to third-party sites.

docs.title: How to Extend CSP (For Modules/Themes)
docs.text: If your module or theme requires loading resources from a new third-party CDN, you should use the system_security_headers_args filter hook.

docs.title: Example: Adding a new Script Origin
docs.text:
Hooks::attach('system_security_headers_args', function($csp_rules) {
    // Add a new CDN to the allowed script sources
    $csp_rules['script-src'][] = "https://my-trusted-cdn.com";

    // Add a new CDN to the allowed style sources
    $csp_rules['style-src'][] = "https://my-trusted-cdn.com";

    return $csp_rules;
});

docs.title: CSRF Protection
docs.text:
GeniXCMS uses a token-based system to prevent Cross-Site Request Forgery. - Token Generation: Handled by the Token class. - Token Validation: Integrated into controllers using Token::validate($_POST['token']). - UI Helper: In the admin dashboard, the TOKEN constant is available for use in forms.

<form action="..." method="post">
    <input type="hidden" name="token" value="<?=TOKEN;?>">
    ...
</form>

[!WARNING] While 'unsafe-inline' and 'unsafe-eval' are currently allowed in the default CSP for compatibility with tools like elFinder and Summernote, it is highly recommended to move toward a nonce-based or hash-based script execution in future custom developments.

docs.title: Troubleshooting CSP Violations
docs.text: If you encounter "Refused to load..." errors in the browser console, it means your theme or module is trying to load a resource from a domain that is not yet in the system whitelist.

docs.title: 1. Resource is Loading via HTTP
docs.text: GeniXCMS defaults to https:// for major CDNs. If you load a script using http://, it may be blocked. - Solution: Always use HTTPS for external assets. - Problematic: <script src="http://cdnjs..."> - Correct: <script src="https://cdnjs...">

docs.title: 2. Missing CDN in Whitelist
docs.text: If you are using a new CDN (e.g., Swiper, AOS, Chart.js), you must register its domain. - Solution: Use the system_security_headers_args hook (see example above).

docs.title: 3. Inline Fonts or Icons
docs.text: Icon libraries (like FontAwesome or Bootstrap Icons) often use data:application/font-woff in their CSS. - Solution: GeniXCMS default policy allows data: for img-src and font-src. If you still see blocks, ensure data: is present in your custom filtered rules.

docs.title: 4. AJAX or API Fetch Errors
docs.text:
If your JavaScript is trying to fetch data from an external API (e.g., Google Maps, Firebase), it will be blocked by connect-src. - Solution: Add the API domain to the connect-src directive using the system_security_headers_args hook.

Hooks::attach('system_security_headers_args', function($rules) {
    $rules['connect-src'][] = "https://api.myapp.com";
    $rules['script-src'][] = "https://cdn.tailwindcss.com";
    return $rules;
});

docs.title: Session Class
docs.text:
# filename: Session.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Session class manages user sessions, supporting both file-based and database-backed storage. It implements SessionHandlerInterface for persistent database sessions.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Session behavior is controlled via constants in inc/config/config.php: - SESSION_DB: If true, sessions are stored in the sessions database table. - SESSION_EXPIRES: Session duration in hours.

docs.title: Start Method
docs.text:
Usage: Session::start(int $duration = 1);

Initializes the session with custom cookie parameters (domain, path, security). It automatically groups system session variables under the $_SESSION['gxsess'] key.

docs.title: Val Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Session::val(string $key);

Retrieves a value from the session's internal val array.

$username = Session::val('username');

docs.title: Set Method
docs.text:
Usage: Session::set(string $key, $value); or Session::set(array $data);

Sets a session variable or multiple variables at once.

Session::set('is_admin', true);
Session::set(['role' => 'editor', 'lang' => 'en']);

docs.title: Remove Method
docs.text:
Usage: Session::remove(string $key);

Unsets a specific variable from the session.

docs.title: Logout Method
docs.text:
Usage: Session::logout();

Destroys the current session and unsets all variables, effectively logging the user out.

docs.title: Database Session Handler
docs.text: When SESSION_DB is enabled, the class provides internal methods for handling the database lifecycle: - open(), close(): Database connection management. - read(), write($data): Reading and writing serialized session data. - destroy(), gc($max): Session cleanup and garbage collection of expired sessions.

docs.title: Site Class
docs.text:
# filename: Site.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

The Site class manages global website properties, SEO metadata, and frontend asset loading. It acts as the primary data container for the current site configuration.

docs.title: Global Properties
docs.text:
The class provides static access to core site information:

  • Site::$name: Site Name.
  • Site::$url: Base Website URL.
  • Site::$desc: Site Description.
  • Site::$slogan: Site Slogan.
  • Site::$email: Admin Email.

docs.title: Meta Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Site::meta(array $data);

Generates all required <meta> tags for the HTML header, including: - Standard meta (charset, generator). - SEO meta (title, description, keywords, robots, canonical). - Social Media meta (Open Graph / Facebook, Twitter Cards).

docs.title: Title / Desc / KeyWords Methods
docs.text:
These helper methods calculate the best SEO values based on the current page data.

echo Site::title($data); // Returns optimized page title
echo Site::desc($content); // Returns truncated meta description

docs.title: JsonLD Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Site::jsonLD(array $data);

Automatically generates JSON-LD structured data (Schema.org) for search engine rich results. Supports BlogPosting for posts and WebPage for pages.

docs.title: Logo Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Site::logo(string $width, string $height, string $class);

Returns the HTML <img> tag for the site logo, handling both local uploads and external URLs.

docs.title: Footer Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Site::footer();

Triggers the footer_load_lib hook and handles developer debug output (if DEBUG is enabled).

docs.title: Minification Methods
docs.text:
• Site::minifyHTML($html);
  • Site::minifyCSS($css);
  • Site::minifyJS($js);

Used for on-the-fly asset optimization.

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

docs.title: Sitemap Class
docs.text:
# filename: Sitemap.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.1
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Sitemap class generates XML-compliant sitemaps for the website to improve search engine indexing. It supports sitemap indexes (splitting sitemaps by category) and individual sitemap lists.

docs.title: Create Method
docs.text:
Usage: Sitemap::create(string $type = 'post', int $count = 20, string $url_method = 'post');

Generates and outputs an XML sitemap directly to the browser. - $type: The content type to include (e.g., 'post', 'page'). - $count: Maximum number of items in the sitemap. - $url_method: The Url class method used to generate links.

// Standard feed for the latest 20 posts
Sitemap::create();

docs.title: CreateIndex Method
docs.text:
Usage: Sitemap::createIndex();

Generates a sitemap index file that lists all category-specific sitemaps. This is the primary entry point for search engine crawlers (e.g., sitemap.xml).

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text:
1. Fetches recent content using Posts::recent().
  2. Sets the Content-Type: text/xml header.
  3. Builds the XML <urlset> or <sitemapindex> structure.
  4. Automatically calculates the lastmod (using the current date) and changefreq (set to daily).

docs.title: Extensibility
docs.text:
Modules can add custom sitemap entries using the addMap() method, though standard post/page types are handled by default:

$my_map = [
    'product' => [
        'class' => 'ProductUrl',
        'url'   => 'detail'
    ]
];
Sitemap::addMap($my_map);

docs.title: Stats Class

The Stats class provides methods for gathering system-wide statistics, including counts for posts, users, categories, and comments. It also handles post view tracking with built-in bot detection.

docs.title: Content Statistics
docs.text:
Methods for counting posts and categories:

  • Stats::totalPost(string $type);: Total posts of a specific type.
  • Stats::activePost(string $type);: Total published posts.
  • Stats::inactivePost(string $type);: Total draft/hidden posts.
  • Stats::totalCat(string $type);: Total categories of a specific type.

docs.title: User Statistics
docs.text:
Methods for monitoring user metrics:

  • Stats::totalUser();: Total registered members (excludes admin).
  • Stats::totalAdmin();: Total administrators.
  • Stats::activeUser();: Total active members.
  • Stats::pendingUser();: Total users awaiting email activation.

docs.title: View Tracking
docs.text: Methods for measuring content popularity:

docs.title: AddViews Method
docs.text:
Usage: Stats::addViews(int $id);

Increments the views counter for a specific post. It automatically ignores most common search engine bots (Googlebot, bingbot, etc.) to ensure accurate human-only metrics.

docs.title: MostViewed Method
docs.text:
Usage: Stats::mostViewed(int $limit, string $type = 'post');

Returns an array of post objects ordered by view count.

docs.title: Comment Statistics
docs.text:
• Stats::totalComments();
  • Stats::activeComments(); (Status 1)
  • Stats::pendingComments(); (Status 2)
  • Stats::inactiveComments(); (Status 0)

docs.title: System Class
docs.text:
# filename: System.class.php
# location: inc/lib/

# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The System class is the bootstrap core of GeniXCMS. It initializes all components and provides low-level system utilities.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text: The constructor initializes the entire framework: 1. Loads configuration. 2. Initiates Database, Hooks, Options, etc. 3. Loads Modules and Themes. 4. Starts the Session. 5. Runs the init hook.

docs.title: Lib Method
docs.text:
Usage: System::lib(string $class_name);

Manually includes a library file from the inc/lib/ directory.

docs.title: V Method (Version)
docs.text:
Usage: echo System::v();

Returns the full system version string.

docs.title: VersionCheck Method
docs.text:
Usage: System::versionCheck();

Triggers an asynchronous check against GitHub for the latest version. If a newer version is found, an alert is attached to the admin dashboard.

docs.title: Alert Method
docs.text:
Usage: System::alert(array $data);

Displays toast notifications in the administrative area.

$data = array(
    'alertSuccess' => array('Settings Saved!'),
    'alertDanger'  => array('Error occurred.')
);
System::alert($data);

docs.title: GZip / Zipped Methods
docs.text: Methods for enabling GZip compression and HTML minification for optimized page delivery.

The Tags class manages post tagging and taxonomy in GeniXCMS. While it leverages the Categories class for its underlying database structure, it provides specific logic for tag manipulation and "tag cloud" generation.

docs.title: Add Method
docs.text:
Usage: Tags::add(string $tags);

Parses a comma-separated string of tags and inserts new ones into the database if they don't already exist.

Tags::add("travel, leisure, lifestyle");

docs.title: Cloud Method
docs.text:
Usage: echo Tags::cloud();

Generates an HTML "Tag Cloud". It: 1. Fetches all categories of type tag. 2. Calculates the frequency of each tag using Tags::count(). 3. Returns a sorted list of linked tags with their post counts.

docs.title: Wrapper Methods (via Categories)
docs.text: Since tags are a subtype of categories, many methods act as wrappers for the Categories class: - Tags::dropdown();: Displays tags in a <select> list. - Tags::name(int $id);: Returns the name of a tag by its ID. - Tags::slug(int $id);: Returns the URL slug of a tag. - Tags::delete(int $id);: Removes a tag from the system.

docs.title: Data Structure
docs.text: Tags are stored in the common cat table with the type field set to 'tag'. This allows them to benefit from the same hierarchical and slug-based systems used by categories.

// Includes inc/themes/mytheme/sidebar.php

docs.title: Token Class
docs.text:
# filename: Token.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS
# since: 0.0.2
# author: Puguh Wijayanto (metalgenix.id)
# copyright: 2014-2024 Puguh Wijayanto
# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Token class provides a Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF) protection layer for GeniXCMS. It generates, stores, and validates unique security tokens linked to specific URLs, IP addresses, and user sessions.

docs.title: TOKEN Constant
docs.text: When the system initializes, a new token is created and stored in the TOKEN global constant. This token should be included in every POST request (forms and Ajax).

docs.title: Create Method
docs.text:
Usage: Token::create();

Generates a new security token. It: 1. Creates a 10-character random string. 2. Captures the current URL, User IP, and Timestamp. 3. Pairs the token with an MD5 hash of the current URL. 4. Stores the token metadata in the tokens system option (JSON encoded).

docs.title: IsValid Method
docs.text:
Usage: Token::isValid(string $token);

Return: bool

Validates a submitted token by checking if it exists in the storage and matches the current request's URL "pairing".

docs.title: Implementation in Forms
docs.text:
To secure a form, include the token as a hidden field:

<form action="save.php" method="POST">
    <input type="hidden" name="token" value="<?=TOKEN;?>">
    ...
</form>

In the receiving controller:

if (!Token::isValid($_POST['token'])) {
    die("Security Error: Invalid Token.");
}

docs.title: Internal Workflow
docs.text:
1. Storage: Tokens are stored in a centralized JSON array within the options table.
  2. Persistence: Tokens are tied to the specific URL that generated them (pairing), preventing a token from one page from being used on another.
  3. Garbage Collection: The ridOld() method automatically clears tokens older than 24 hours to prevent the options table from bloating.

Info

The Typo class provides utilities for text manipulation, input sanitization, security filtering, and cryptographic operations. It is used extensively throughout the CMS to ensure data integrity and security.

docs.title: CleanX Method
docs.text:
Usage: Typo::cleanX(string $input);

The primary input sanitizer. It: 1. Removes <script> tags. 2. Applies HTMLPurifier for robust XSS protection. 3. Encodes HTML special characters. 4. Backslashes backslashes for database safety.

docs.title: Xclean Method
docs.text:
Usage: Typo::Xclean(string $input);

Reverses cleanX for display in forms or editors by decoding HTML entities.

docs.title: FilterXss Method
docs.text:
Usage: Typo::filterXss(string $input);

Removes dangerous HTML attributes (like onload, onclick) and javascript: URIs from strings.

docs.title: Formatting Methods
docs.text:
• Typo::slugify(string $text);: Converts a string to a URL-friendly slug.
  • Typo::nl2p(string $text);: Converts newlines to <p> tags.
  • Typo::url2link(string $text);: Automatically detects URLs in text and wraps them in <a> tags.
  • Typo::p2nl(string $text);: Converts <p> and <br> tags back to newlines.

docs.title: Cryptography & Tokens
docs.text:
• Typo::getToken(int $length);: Generates a random alphanumeric token.
  • Typo::createToken(int $length, bool $cap, bool $low, bool $num, bool $symbol);: Advanced token generator with character set control.
  • Typo::crypto_rand_secure(int $min, int $max);: Generates a cryptographically secure random integer.

docs.title: Validation & Utilities
docs.text:
• Typo::validateEmail(string $email);: Returns true if the email format is valid.
  • Typo::int($var);: Ensures a variable is an integer.
  • Typo::float($var);: Formats a variable as a float with 2 decimal places.
  • Typo::translate(string $original);: Wrapper for the global _() gettext localization function.

docs.title: UiBuilder Class
docs.text: The UiBuilder class is a high-level, generic layout engine in GeniXCMS v2.0+ designed to build complex, responsive administrative interfaces (dashboards, forms, tables, and stat cards) using simple arrays (schemas).

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• Dynamic Headers: Automatic sticky header with breadcrumb, icons, and primary action buttons.
  • Nested Grid System: Supports multi-column rows (gx-grid-n) and cards.
  • Modern ERP Aesthetic: Standardized compact design with Bootstrap 5 integration.
  • Flexible Elements: Support for stats cards, alerts, switch toggles, raw HTML, and tabbed sub-pages.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
To use the UiBuilder in your backend module view:

// 1. Define the Schema
$schema = [
    'header' => [
        'title'    => _('User Management'),
        'subtitle' => _('Complete list of users in the ecosystem.'),
        'icon'     => 'bi bi-people-fill',
        'button'   => [
            'type' => 'button', 'name' => 'save_user', 'label' => _('Create User'),
            'icon' => 'bi bi-plus-lg', 'class' => 'btn btn-primary rounded-pill'
        ]
    ],
    'content' => [
        ['type' => 'row', 'items' => [
            ['width' => 12, 'content' => [
                ['type' => 'card', 'body_elements' => [
                    ['type' => 'input', 'name' => 'username', 'label' => _('User ID')],
                    ['type' => 'input', 'name' => 'email', 'label' => _('Email Address'), 'input_type' => 'email']
                ]]
            ]]
        ]]
    ]
];

// 2. Render the Interface
$builder = new UiBuilder($schema);
$builder->render();

docs.title: Element Types
docs.text: Element Description row A layout container that holds items (columns). card A Bootstrap 5 card container with title, icon, and body_elements. input Standard form input (text, password, email, number). switch Boolean On/Off toggle. select Dropdown Menu. alert Contextual notification block (info, warning, danger, success). stats_card Dashboard metric card with value, label, icon, and trend indicator. raw / html Injection of custom HTML content.

docs.title: __construct(array $schema = [])
docs.text: Initializes a new builder instance with the provided schema.

docs.title: render()
docs.text: Outputs the full responsive HTML for the interface.

docs.title: renderHeader()
docs.text: Only renders the header section (useful for custom page layouts).

docs.title: renderTabs()
docs.text: Renders the navigation tabs defined in the schema.

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Naming Buttons: Always specify a custom name in the button array to avoid collisions during form submission.
  2. Security: Wrap the render() call in a <form> and include the security token: 
    echo '<form method="POST">';
$builder->render();
echo '<input type="hidden" name="token" value="'.TOKEN.'">';
echo '</form>';
  3. Sticky Header: Ensure your content has enough bottom padding to prevent the footer from overlapping the header on long pages.

docs.title: Upload Class
docs.text:
# filename: Upload.class.php
# location: inc/lib/
# package: GeniXCMS

# license: MIT License

Info

This Documentation is still need improvement.

The Upload class provides a simple and secure interface for handling file uploads in GeniXCMS. It includes support for extension filtering and unique file naming.

docs.title: Go Method
docs.text:
Usage: $result = Upload::go(string $input, string $path, array $allowed, bool $uniq = false);

Processes an uploaded file. - $input: The name of the file input field in the HTML form. - $path: The destination directory relative to GX_PATH (e.g., assets/uploads/). - $allowed: An array of permitted file extensions (lowercase). - $uniq: If true, generates a unique name for the file.

$allowed = ['jpg', 'jpeg', 'png', 'gif'];
$res = Upload::go('site_logo', 'assets/images/', $allowed, true);

if (isset($res['error'])) {
    echo "Upload failed: " . $res['error'];
} else {
    echo "Uploaded to: " . $res['fileurl'];
}

docs.title: Return Values
docs.text: If successful, the method returns an array containing: - filesize: Size of the uploaded file in bytes. - filename: The final name of the file (including unique prefix if requested). - path: The relative path to the file. - filepath: The full absolute storage path. - fileurl: The full public URL to access the file.

docs.title: Security Features
docs.text:
1. Filename Sanitization: Cleans the original filename using Typo::cleanX and replaces spaces with underscores.
  2. Extension Filtering: Strictly enforces the $allowed extensions list.
  3. Unique Naming: Optional generation of cryptographically secure unique filenames to prevent overwriting or guessing.

The User class handles all user-related operations, including authentication, registration, profiles, and permission management.

docs.title: User Groups
docs.text:
GeniXCMS uses a numeric role system:

  • 0: Administrator
  • 1: Supervisor
  • 2: Editor
  • 3: Author
  • 4: Contributor
  • 5: VIP Member
  • 6: General Member (Default)

docs.title: Secure Method
docs.text:
Usage: User::secure();

Ensures the user is logged in. If not, it redirects them to the login page with a backto return URL.

docs.title: Access Method
docs.text:
Usage: User::access(string $grp = '6');

Checks if the currently logged-in user has a group level equal to or better than (smaller numeric value) the specified group.

if (User::access('0')) {
    echo "Welcome, Admin!";
}

docs.text:
Usage: User::isLoggedin();

Return: bool

Checks if a user session is active.

The Vendor class manages third-party libraries (Composer-based and others) installed within GeniXCMS. It provides methods for autoloading and retrieving library assets.

docs.title: Autoload Method
docs.text:
Usage: Vendor::autoload();

Includes the main Composer autoload.php located in inc/lib/Vendor/. This is called during system initialization to make external packages available for use.

docs.title: Loadonce Method
docs.text:
Usage: Vendor::loadonce(string $file_path);

Specifically requires a file from within the Vendor/ directory. Useful for non-Composer compatible libraries.

Vendor::loadonce('ezyang/htmlpurifier/library/HTMLPurifier.auto.php');

docs.title: Path & URL Utilities
docs.text:
• Vendor::url();: Returns the public URL path to the vendor directory, typically used for including CSS or JS files from libraries like Bootstrap, elFinder, or FontAwesome.
  • Vendor::path(string $package_name);: Returns the internal server path to a specific package directory.

docs.title: Included Libraries
docs.text: GeniXCMS relies on several enterprise-grade libraries managed by this class, including: - PHPMailer: For email sending. - HTMLPurifier: For robust XSS protection. - Summernote: For the WYSIWYG editor. - Intervention Image: For dynamic image processing. - Latte: For the frontend templating engine.

docs.text:
